next_tickets.body: Question: is the S19a winning architecture limited by training support rather than architecture capacity? Run source-run-held-out augmentation and shallow ensembling only for the S19a winner, with the same analytic/template baselines and bootstrap guard metrics. Expected information gain: separates model-capacity gains from synthetic-support artifacts before any production adoption.
next_tickets.title: S19b: support-preserving augmentation and ensemble check for the architecture-sweep winner

scientific_summary: Timing point-estimate winner is mlp at 1.176 ns versus analytic_timewalk 1.495 ns. Two-pulse point-estimate winner is gradient_boosted_trees at 7.425 ns versus constrained_template_fit 13.874 ns. The winner named here is the held-out metric winner; adoption remains conditional on the failure-rate and leakage guards documented in REPORT.md.
